# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a TypeScript Express endpoint for serving user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via req.params.filename. Use fs.readFile with path.join to read from uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization. Type the filename parameter as string. This is for our file storage API.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Canonicalize and validate file paths to prevent path traversal.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Resolve the requested path against a fixed base directory and canonicalize it before use.
- Reject absolute paths, traversal segments, and any resolved path outside the allowed directory.
- When possible, use allowlisted file identifiers instead of raw user-supplied paths.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not join a user filename directly to a base directory and open it without validation.
- Do not rely only on checking whether ".." appears in the string.
# Category: path_traversal

import express, { Request, Response, Router } from "express";
import fs from "fs";
import path from "path";

const router: Router = express.Router();

// Fixed base directory for uploads - resolve to absolute path once at startup
const UPLOADS_BASE_DIR: string = path.resolve(__dirname, "uploads");

/**
 * Validates and canonicalizes a user-supplied filename against the uploads base directory.
 * Returns the safe, resolved absolute path or null if the path is invalid/malicious.
 */
function resolveAndValidateFilePath(filename: string): string | null {
  // Reject empty or whitespace-only filenames
  if (!filename || filename.trim().length === 0) {
    return null;
  }

  // Reject absolute paths (Unix and Windows styles)
  if (path.isAbsolute(filename)) {
    return null;
  }

  // Reject null bytes which can truncate paths in some systems
  if (filename.includes("\0")) {
    return null;
  }

  // Reject paths containing traversal segments before resolution
  // Split on both forward and back slashes to catch all variations
  const segments = filename.split(/[/\\]/);
  for (const segment of segments) {
    if (segment === ".." || segment === ".") {
      return null;
    }
  }

  // Reject backslashes entirely to prevent Windows-style traversal on any platform
  if (filename.includes("\\")) {
    return null;
  }

  // Validate each path segment contains only safe characters
  // Allow alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, dots (but not leading dots), and forward slashes for subdirectories
  const safeFilenamePattern = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-][a-zA-Z0-9_\-.]*(\/[a-zA-Z0-9_\-][a-zA-Z0-9_\-.]*)*$/;
  if (!safeFilenamePattern.test(filename)) {
    return null;
  }

  // Resolve the full path by joining with the base directory
  const resolvedPath: string = path.resolve(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR, filename);

  // Canonicalize and verify the resolved path is strictly within the uploads directory
  // The resolved path must start with the base directory followed by a path separator
  // This is the critical check that prevents any traversal that slipped through
  if (!resolvedPath.startsWith(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR + path.sep) && resolvedPath !== UPLOADS_BASE_DIR) {
    return null;
  }

  return resolvedPath;
}

/**
 * GET /files/:filename
 * Serves user-uploaded files by name. Supports subdirectories (e.g., /files/images/photo.png).
 * The :filename parameter captures the rest of the path including slashes.
 */
router.get("/files/*", (req: Request, res: Response): void => {
  // Extract the filename from the wildcard parameter (supports subdirectories)
  const filename: string = req.params[0] as string;

  if (!filename) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "Filename is required" });
    return;
  }

  // Validate and canonicalize the file path
  const safePath: string | null = resolveAndValidateFilePath(filename);

  if (safePath === null) {
    // Log the attempt for security monitoring (avoid logging the raw filename to prevent log injection)
    console.warn(
      `Rejected file access attempt from IP: ${req.ip}, requested path did not pass validation`
    );
    res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid filename" });
    return;
  }

  // Check that the file exists and is a regular file (not a directory or symlink)
  fs.stat(safePath, (statErr: NodeJS.ErrnoException | null, stats: fs.Stats) => {
    if (statErr) {
      if (statErr.code === "ENOENT") {
        res.status(404).json({ error: "File not found" });
      } else {
        console.error(`Error accessing file: ${statErr.message}`);
        res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error" });
      }
      return;
    }

    // Reject directories and symlinks - only serve regular files
    if (!stats.isFile()) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid filename" });
      return;
    }

    // Also verify the real path after symlink resolution is still within the base directory
    fs.realpath(safePath, (realpathErr: NodeJS.ErrnoException | null, realPath: string) => {
      if (realpathErr) {
        console.error(`Error resolving real path: ${realpathErr.message}`);
        res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error" });
        return;
      }

      if (!realPath.startsWith(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR + path.sep) && realPath !== UPLOADS_BASE_DIR) {
        console.warn(`Symlink traversal attempt detected from IP: ${req.ip}`);
        res.status(400).json({ error: "Invalid filename" });
        return;
      }

      // Read and serve the file
      fs.readFile(realPath, (readErr: NodeJS.ErrnoException | null, data: Buffer) => {
        if (readErr) {
          if (readErr.code === "ENOENT") {
            res.status(404).json({ error: "File not found" });
          } else {
            console.error(`Error reading file: ${readErr.message}`);
            res.status(500).json({ error: "Internal server error" });
          }
          return;
        }

        // Determine content type from extension
        const ext: string = path.extname(realPath).toLowerCase();
        const contentTypeMap: Record<string, string> = {
          ".html": "text/html",
          ".css": "text/css",
          ".js": "application/javascript",
          ".json": "application/json",
          ".png": "image/png",
          ".jpg": "image/jpeg",
          ".jpeg": "image/jpeg",
          ".gif": "image/gif",
          ".svg": "image/svg+xml",
          ".pdf": "application/pdf",
          ".txt": "text/plain",
          ".xml": "application/xml",
          ".zip": "application/zip",
          ".mp4": "video/mp4",
          ".mp3": "audio/mpeg",
          ".webp": "image/webp",
          ".ico": "image/x-icon",
        };

        const contentType: string = contentTypeMap[ext] || "application/octet-stream";

        // Set security headers
        res.setHeader("Content-Type", contentType);
        res.setHeader("Content-Length", data.length);
        res.setHeader("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff");
        res.setHeader("Content-Disposition", `inline; filename="${path.basename(realPath)}"`);

        res.status(200).send(data);
      });
    });
  });
});

// Create and configure the Express app
const app = express();

// Ensure the uploads directory exists
if (!fs.existsSync(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR)) {
  fs.mkdirSync(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR, { recursive: true });
}

app.use(router);

const PORT: number = parseInt(process.env.PORT || "3000", 10);
app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`File storage API server running on port ${PORT}`);
  console.log(`Serving files from: ${UPLOADS_BASE_DIR}`);
});

export { router, resolveAndValidateFilePath, UPLOADS_BASE_DIR };
export default app;
